items.content: Auth service latency spike correlates exactly with DB pool exhaustion events documented in stats-realistic. Same root cause: retry storms without backoff.
Session token not invalidated on logout — same root cause as cache race condition documented in schema-v0 journal.
Consolidate all auth findings into a dedicated auth-deep-dive journal. Cross-reference items will remain here for traceability.
OAuth token refresh endpoint bypasses rate limiter — refresh storms possible when many clients lose tokens simultaneously.
Rate limiter config is loaded at process startup only — live changes require restart. Hot-reload needed.
Add config hot-reload via SIGHUP for rate limiter. Atomic swap of config pointer to avoid races during reload.
HTTP client timeout context pattern documented in correction-trail journal is directly applicable to OAuth refresh client.
JWT expiry set to 5 minutes — too short, causes refresh storms when many sessions expire simultaneously at peak.
Extend JWT expiry to 15 minutes. Combine with sliding window refresh to avoid hard expiry cliffs.
All auth findings summarised. Key remediations: retry backoff, session invalidation, rate limiter hot-reload, JWT expiry extension. Handoff to auth-deep-dive journal.
